children.name: TriggerBuild
children.purpose: Create a new build record and start the build process
children.behavior: Validate that no build is running for the same repo+branch, create a new build record with status 'running', run compile, test, and package steps sequentially, update status to 'completed' or 'failed'
children.signature: def TriggerBuild(build_request: dict, builds_db: Database) -> dict

global_vars.description: Read existing builds to check for duplicates, write new build records and update status/logs
Write artifact metadata when build completes successfully

data_operations.description: Read to check for running builds, write to create/update build records
Write artifact metadata after successful build

constraints.description: Must not trigger a build if a build with same repo+branch is already running
Build steps (compile, test, package) must execute sequentially

acceptance_criteria.description: New build record created with status 'running'
Build status updated to 'completed' or 'failed' after steps

children.name: CheckBuildStatus
children.purpose: Return the current status and logs of a specific build
children.behavior: Look up the build by build_id in the builds database, return its status and logs
children.signature: def CheckBuildStatus(build_request: dict, builds_db: Database) -> dict

data_operations.description: Read build record to retrieve status and logs

constraints.description: Must return error if build_id does not exist

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns current status and logs for existing build_id

children.name: ListBuildHistory
children.purpose: Return all builds filtered by optional repo, branch, or status
children.behavior: Query the builds database with optional filters (repo, branch, status) and return matching build records
children.signature: def ListBuildHistory(build_request: dict, builds_db: Database) -> dict

global_vars.description: Read all builds and apply filters

data_operations.description: Read builds collection and filter by provided criteria

constraints.description: Filters (repo, branch, status) are optional; if none provided, return all builds

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns list of builds matching provided filters
Returns all builds if no filters provided

children.name: CancelBuild
children.purpose: Stop a running build and update its status to cancelled
children.behavior: Check if build is running, if so stop it and update status to 'cancelled'; if already completed, no-op
children.signature: def CancelBuild(build_request: dict, builds_db: Database) -> dict

global_vars.description: Read build status, write updated status to 'cancelled'

data_operations.description: Read build record to check status, write updated status if running

constraints.description: Only running builds can be cancelled; completed/failed builds are no-op
Must terminate underlying build process if running

governance_notes: All children inherit parent's global_vars but only use subsets as specified. TriggerBuild and CancelBuild require write access to builds; CheckBuildStatus and ListBuildHistory require only read access. Artifacts write is only needed by TriggerBuild on successful completion. Process execution/termination capabilities are requested only by TriggerBuild and CancelBuild respectively.
